summary: Tested 401 error handling fix for Customer Account Page. All tests pass - backend returns proper 401 errors for invalid/expired tokens, and frontend correctly handles these errors by clearing localStorage and redirecting to homepage with a toast message.
critical_code_review_comments: handleAuthError function in CustomerAccountPage.jsx is properly implemented - clears localStorage and redirects to homepage
All API calls (fetchData, refreshOrders, handleSaveProfile) correctly check for 401 status and call handleAuthError
Email templates in email_service.py have clean premium design with good visual hierarchy
context_for_next_testing_agent: 401 error handling for customer account page has been fully tested and verified working. Backend endpoints return 401 for invalid/expired tokens, frontend redirects to homepage and shows 'Your session has expired. Please login again.' toast message.

401_error_handling_backend.details: All customer endpoints (/api/customer/orders, /api/customer/stats, /api/auth/customer/profile, /api/auth/customer/me) return 401 for invalid/expired tokens

401_error_handling_frontend.details: When 401 error is received on CustomerAccountPage, localStorage is cleared (customer_token and customer_info removed) and user is redirected to homepage

toast_notification.details: Toast message 'Your session has expired. Please login again.' is displayed when session expires

no_auth_redirect.details: Navigating to /account without any token properly redirects to homepage

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Customer account page showing 'couldn't fetch data' error when token expired
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Added handleAuthError function that clears localStorage and redirects to homepage with toast message
bug_fix_verification.test_method: Set invalid token in localStorage, navigate to /account, verified redirect to homepage and localStorage cleared
